_comment: WORKED EXAMPLE — the live-demo specimen for Exercise Three. This is the instructor's own targets.json, performed in class. Students produce their own version against their own record. Schema is two-layered on purpose: the MACHINE LAYER is captured + mapped (extraction is fluent, the machine is better at it); the HUMAN LAYER is selected + annotated + signed (only the student knows which O*NET code is right, which 3 requirements matter, and why this role). See docs/exercises/exercise-03-target-gap-prd-recipe.md.

targets.title: Machine Learning Platform Engineer
targets._layer_machine: captured + SOC/O*NET-mapped by the agent — no judgment
requirements_full: Experience building and operating ML pipelines in production
Collaboration with research scientists; clear written communication

alt_considered.why_not_chosen: Posting is platform/infra-weighted, not analysis-weighted — Systems Engineer fits the verbatim requirements better. Human call; agent had proposed Data Scientist.

targets._layer_human: selected + annotated + signed by the student

top_3.requirement: Experience building and operating ML pipelines in production
top_3.why_top: Named first in the responsibilities section and repeated in 'what you'll do' — the load-bearing ask.
Directly buildable in Madison — this is where my gap and my coursework meet.
The Mycroft audit discipline is exactly this; my strongest transferable claim.

targets.why_this_role: It sits where my teaching work and my systems instinct overlap — building the reliable plumbing research depends on, not the research itself.
targets.soc_attested_note: Confirmed 15-1299.08 against the actual posting; agent had proposed Data Scientist (15-2051.00) and I corrected it — the posting is infra-weighted.

hiring_manager.public_facts: Team led by a platform/infra lead per the posting; no individual named.
hiring_manager._rule: Public facts only, source cited. No speculation, no contact details. If unsure, leave null or move to brand/private-reflection.md.

canonical_profile._comment: Derived view — agent-computed, student-attested. EMPTY in week one (one target). It fills as later sessions add listings; N listings mapped to the same SOC/O*NET codes converge into the lane's real demands. This is the multi-week payoff: one posting is an anecdote, a SOC-keyed aggregate is data.
canonical_profile.note: One data point is not a profile. This becomes meaningful at 4+ listings — that's the arc, not this week.
